textByHash.7cdc71d4c00cb47fd6e6808b5d6fbc535f9d38f3: Ah... I take it you are one of the new recruits?
textByHash.016867e8f8827fb06b8c2e357fe40686539f663d: I'm Essyllt and I'm in charge here. Now come on, let us have a look at your paper work.
textByHash.12845066825ef30484df9dd504434fc5c9aaa9f0: This seems to be all in order. Welcome to the Death Guard. Now, for your first assignment... As you may know, part of what we do here is keep the people believing in the plague...
textByHash.c45c370fcf893c06e4ba7662599662eddd64b7a2: Did they not tell you? Hmm... I'd better fill you in. Well as you undoubtedly know, we were sent here by Lord Iorwerth to secure the city. The reason for this is so that we can access the caves below...
textByHash.d8d4c94427b3b6959587090658d8b25eb1ae3a07: I'm afraid that information is classified until you prove your commitment to the Death Guard.
textByHash.19307fce164dc21b6a0934c1a46d915748c9e918: Now to keep our presence in the city secret, Lord Iorwerth arranged an alliance with King Lathas, the ruler in the east. Apparently the fool thinks we will help him dispose of the Knights of Camelot once our work here is done. I expect he'll be disappointed. Anyway, the plague serves two purposes. First, it allows us to move around the city in secret thanks to our mourner disguises. Second, it means we can abduct citizens to mine the caves below with ease. We just pretend they've fallen victim to the plague.
textByHash.2a6422251611b07e1bd74a70a0b24428b5e39f74: Well we have had a few near misses. Recently we had some girl from East Ardougne poking around asking too many questions, so we locked her up while we decided what to do with her. Annoyingly, she managed to escape with the help of some adventurer. Lucky for us, they were so stupid that we managed to use the adventurer to deal with King Tyras. It all worked out quite well in the end. Things like that is why we must ensure the people keep believing in the plague though. One of the things we do to keep up the lie is to fool old Farmer Brumty into believing his sheep are infected. The old halfwit thinks just because his sheep are an abnormal colour, that they are all ill with plague. It's amazing what a bit of dye can do.
textByHash.763209729ac2885b44583acf0eff3b9e9915a795: Well you don't think they end up those ridiculous colours naturally do you? Anyway, shame of it is that we have yet to find a way to stop the dye from washing out. So we need someone, that someone being you, to go and re-dye them.
textByHash.3284413535a26726ccf8bc0aa25a7407e91331b6: Not quite, for starters the sheep need to be dyed red, yellow, green and blue. But most important is that you are not seen doing this by anyone. Also you will need to re-dye them the colours they already are or the farmer may notice the change.
textByHash.3688d7b1fe0f3d5f2e8f57b1b9d029e91e7cbb22: We have a gnomic device that fires fat dye parcels that rupture on impact. Unfortunately, we have run out of the parcels and the device is broken.
textByHash.ff011b80d2f8d48e486b3d1763e7f72d5348ce7c: Sure. We have a gnome inventor here too. Sadly, he is not being very helpful about fixing it, but you can talk to him if you like. Here is the key, he is in the next room. And here's the device as well.
textByHash.42543e4c29331afcbe52c388ef54adcd6cf37d3e: Have you finished with those sheep yet?
textByHash.fcf229250c0b29dac3ad1cc876591d728fe218cc: Well it is lucky we have a whole stack of broken devices, there must be some flaw in the design, they always break in the same way. If you ever need another there is a chest full of them over there.
textByHash.26cd5173671c4682c77ef084c787cac3994846c1: I also keep copies of the key on my desk so I can replace that too.
textByHash.73d9208523ab2c323b7cb1500a2fc03b9ead1a16: Well it is lucky we have a whole stack of broken ones, there must be some flaw in the design, they always break in the same way. If you ever need another there is a chest full of them over there.
textByHash.5a86f890b26b66681ad8ccf45d70f09e4266abf2: I keep copies of the key on my desk so I can replace that.
textByHash.bd35897e779c95c73d05a019ae08621e3ba88d0d: It is good to see your enthusiasm. I was going to get one of the others to do this job, but as you are here... It has been quite some time since anyone got ill form the plague, so I would like you to see to it that people do!
textByHash.5a85089b0e5dacc7a7dab493033b00bcf3b581df: We have never tried this before, but some joker put something in our food not too long ago that gave us all symptoms akin to those of the plague. If you can find out what it was that we were poisoned with, you could reproduce the effects. If done right the poison should not be fatal and could help restock our dwindling supply of cheap labour. We can have our men remove the 'infected' citizens and sent into the mines. Distribution should be easy enough. Because of the city walls no one can grow their own food. Instead, all the food here comes from one of three supply points.
textByHash.aea30365c74ea4a224d00b1b650b27b8b3bff910: Don't forget the part where you use the poison to contaminate the food supply. Two of the three supply points should be enough.
textByHash.f1d1cc8db1e28ba4d73769fb57a03ac89ab6079f: You seem resourceful so I'm sure you will come up with something. I would help you but I am not a biologist.
textByHash.9b0d3e5819f87f8a2ab1d3d78b2331e99a634b04: Very well. Perform this task and then return to me.
textByHash.775214586c237afcfc73a5562e81c6602bf4c0b2: You are back already? How is the epidemic going?
textByHash.cf6ff89c847b9a5971e5bafb90b48295902c1e14: Subtle as a brick... Yes that, how is it going?
textByHash.31411e89557bc850f19e242625f1cfa96e24e517: This is good news, give it a few days and the slave pens will be full again.
textByHash.568ee372995f68f2a36bf1b020cee8726d458617: Yes I think you have. I guess you want to know what we're doing in the mines?
textByHash.c7393aa868e053104b6c96bc659d08318aad70f9: A long time ago, Seren herself ordered the construction of a great temple deep beneath the earth. This temple guards a great power, one that is key to our plans. We believe the temple lies beneath this city. That is why we are here. Unfortunately, progress has been slower than we'd have liked. The slaves accidently mined into some old caverns infested with beasts. They have caused us significant issues.
textByHash.7a52bcd7efa0d30106888cb255a73999bcd32a5a: It shouldn't be long now. We have started to see signs that we are near. Anyway, now that you know about the temple, I have a new task for you to perform, deep within the mines. Alas, one of the guards has taken the key to the mines to be copied. Report in regularly and I will see that you get a copy as soon as he gets back.
textByHash.0a2e721248cc0c41d8ffa2ff8527fe20a0f7d507: Now be on your way, I have other things to attend to.
textByHash.a0b1f0ac88b83ade0643e91bff6bccb7c6b67fc5: No, the guard is being a bit slow, but we should have it soon.
textByHash.cb7566437c2e1ad79ce279429256c67f352b566b: Good... We've been expecting you. King Lathas sent word of your coming.
textByHash.2def06f76b106a52f8d2b9067b4997fede11d559: You should speak with Lord Iorwerth. He will help you track down that brigand Tyras. You'll find his camp in the north west of the forest.
textByHash.fcd482e0d7e0b5f583a203bb2abc449af787ebdc: You! I knew you were trouble! Well don't worry, Lord Iorwerth will learn of your betrayal!
textByHash.df78980bc0b7b652544bfea0057a865d5fde0875: We should have killed them both when we had the chance.
textByHash.e35a2ef3ae2dfacdf4141c9993b51e6819a11304: Let me deal with them, my lord. They've caused us enough trouble.
textByHash.ba2924f24de6ae5df4aa9f3c25ace380574e55c6: Ha! Looks like you were too slow my friend.
textByHash.914535b8da9dc2be3f8ad32d55190a54c01702fb: Or what? Will you murder me in cold blood like you did with King Tyras?
textByHash.be9d4ab0778495e57bc4e0b7bf5f61c758d7259f: You can lie to yourself all you want, I know what you are. You enjoy killing, just like I do.
textByHash.7ab0d62d005655b7e4a129849b35e0426b8abfe7: The satisfaction when I finally put an end to that fool Ysgawyn, it was beautiful. It was even better than when I dealt with that stupid lover of his.
textByHash.8e9ace33372fb61baa6c6d24b12e1c94ac439e03: But if that wasn't enough, now I get to be the one to finally put an end to you and your pathetic girlfriend!
textByHash.a13b98928ab526f2f6aa1cb4fbd8c362439cb80e: Oh I'm going to enjoy this...
